purpose: Make the main OSS-support and reviewer-program fit criteria explicit so outside evaluators can see why this public specs surface is credible today without implying acceptance, traction, or hosted-runtime maturity that the repo does not yet prove.

fit_families.summary: The repository presents a clear maintainer-owned public surface with explicit ownership, scope, and contributor-facing governance.
fit_families.why_it_matters: Programs and reviewers need to see that this is a real maintained public repository surface rather than an anonymous dump or one-off export.
bounded_fit_claims: public scope and repository purpose are explicit
maintainer-owned release and review posture is visible
bounded_non_claims: does not claim a large maintainer team today
does not claim existing broad external governance participation

fit_families.summary: The repository provides a bounded reviewer path through docs, machine-readable summaries, and public governance surfaces.
fit_families.why_it_matters: Programs need a fast, legible route to evaluate what is published, what is deferred, and which artifacts are strongest.
bounded_fit_claims: external reviewers can follow a bounded fast path
public governance and review surfaces are legible without scraping the whole tree
reviewer shortcuts are explicit and machine-readable
bounded_non_claims: does not claim every possible reviewer question is pre-answered
does not replace stronger source contracts with reviewer summaries

fit_families.summary: The repository exposes machine-readable provenance, verification coverage, and release evidence instead of relying only on prose claims.
fit_families.why_it_matters: Program reviewers often need confidence that the public surface is checked, synchronized, and not merely documented.
bounded_fit_claims: verification coverage is explicit and reviewable
public artifact discovery and provenance are tracked
bounded_non_claims: does not claim formal certification beyond repo-local verification
does not replace live validator behavior with summary layers

fit_families.summary: The repository exposes concrete public utility through schema, OpenAPI, examples, recipes, and explicit external-value surfaces.
fit_families.why_it_matters: A support program needs to see that the repo can help integrators, tool-builders, contributors, and reviewers today, not only in theory.
bounded_fit_claims: external utility is described in bounded, artifact-backed terms
examples and recipes make the repo usable beyond prose
bounded_non_claims: does not claim current large-scale external adoption
does not treat recipes as proof of deployed hosted-service interoperability

fit_families.summary: The repository makes hardening history, change classes, update coherence, and release discipline explicit.
fit_families.why_it_matters: Programs need to see that the public surface is being actively shaped and maintained with deliberate release discipline.
bounded_fit_claims: active hardening is visible as public milestones
co-moving summary and release surfaces are governed
bounded_non_claims: does not claim long multi-year public maintenance history
does not treat one release posture document as proof of future release discipline

fit_families.summary: The repository explicitly publishes limits, evidence gaps, non-claims, and bounded maturity posture instead of implying more than it can prove.
fit_families.why_it_matters: Programs often reject public surfaces that read as overclaimed or vague about missing proof and deferred scope.
bounded_fit_claims: deferred scope and non-claims are explicit
program-facing fit remains bounded by stronger release evidence
bounded_non_claims: does not claim hosted-runtime maturity, traction, or service evidence that the repo does not yet publish

non_claims: This file does not claim acceptance by OpenAI or any other OSS-support program.
This file does not replace stronger contracts, provenance, release evidence, or future public adoption signals.
